item.title: Crypto Money Laundering
item.description: Crypto Money Laundering: Definition, Stages, and Common Techniques Money laundering is the process of concealing illegally obtained funds to make them appear legitimate. In crypto, laundering has evolved because digital assets can move quickly across borders and between platforms, often with limited identity context unless strong compliance controls are in place. Crypto doesn’t make laundering […]

item.title: The Ransomware Ecosystem: Roles, Tools, and How Modern RaaS Attacks Work
item.description: Ransomware is no longer just a malicious program deployed by a single attacker — it has evolved into a complex, profit-driven ecosystem operating much like a legitimate business model. Today’s ransomware operations involve multiple specialized actors, automated platforms, and underground services working together to maximize impact and financial gain. From Initial Access Brokers selling compromised […]

item.title: Smishing Triad Targets Egypt’s Financial Sector and Postal Services
item.description: Recently, during one of our threat hunting operations, our squad identified multiple malicious domains impersonating major Egyptian service providers, including Fawry, the Egypt Post, and Careem. These domains were likely established to support fraud, phishing campaigns, and other malicious activities targeting users and organizations. Before we begin our analysis, we will provide an overview of […]
